name: CGSTL Jilin-1 (fleet)

overview.value: The Jilin-1 satellite project is Chang Guang Satellite Technology's core in-development program. The company says that through 30 successful launches it has put a cumulative total of 161 Jilin-1 constellation satellites into orbit, building what it describes as the world's largest sub-meter commercial remote sensing satellite constellation, with the ability to update 0.5-meter resolution nationwide imagery on a quarterly cycle.

notes: CGSTL site lists Jilin-1 satellite models (GXA, SP04-06, GP01/02, KF01B, GF03D01-03) but no totals; HQ stated as Changchun, Jilin, China. Converted to a fleet parent (2026-07-05): CGSTL's Jilin-1 program nests its catalogued series (Gaofen, Kuanfu, early/video) as children. Checked 2026-07-09: sats_active_claimed stays null. CGSTL's own site (jl1.cn) does not publish a currently-active figure distinct from its cumulative-launched count (161, unchanged); it also has not published anything more recent than 161. GCAT (planet4589.org/space/con/jil/stats.html) offers a computed 'total in orbit: 89 / total working: 88' as of 2026-07-05, but its Jilin taxonomy does not cleanly reconcile with CGSTL's own 161 branding (GCAT's broader Jilin catalog totals 190 launched, including non-Jilin-1-branded payloads) — flagged for Florian rather than entered.

events.headline: Chang Guang Satellite Technology established as China's first commercial remote sensing satellite company
Chang Guang launches first Jilin-1 satellites, opening China's commercial satellite era
Chang Guang raises 2.46 billion yuan pre-IPO round for Jilin-1 program
Chang Guang expands Jilin-1 constellation target from 138 to 300 satellites
Jilin-1 program reaches 30th launch, bringing constellation to 117 satellites
